problem.instruction: Identify the ONE category in which Mr. Kim's comments do NOT correspond to student 1's restaurant review, and the ONE category in which Mr. Kim's comments do NOT correspond to student 2's restaurant review. Then explain your answers with evidence.
problem.passage:
Student 1's review: Recently I visited Bamgart, a Chinese restaurant. There were so many dishes on the menu. I ordered seafood noodle soup. It was spicy. It was very delicious. It took some time to order.
Grade B / Content: Good to Fair / Organization: Good to Fair / Grammar: Excellent
Comments: You did a good job on writing the restaurant review. You addressed the topic well by elaborating it with details. Also, your ideas were smoothly connected using appropriate linking words, but you did not include a concluding sentence. There were no major grammatical errors that obscured the meaning. Keep up the good work!

Student 2's review: I recently had a lunch at Pizza Club. I ordered a combination pizza. I liked the taste and the reasonable price, but I have to wait long outside before being seated. Restaurant was very crowded, so I eat the pizza quickly. Overall, it was worth visiting this place.
Grade B / Content: Excellent / Organization: Excellent / Grammar: Good to Fair
Comments: I enjoyed reading your writing. The message was very clear, and it was good to see the details you included. You made good connections between your ideas using appropriate linking words, so it was easy to follow your writing. Regarding grammar, you had some errors in the use of articles, but everything else was good in terms of accuracy. Nice work!

steps.label: 발문 조건 확인
steps.explanation: 각 학생에서 '점수/등급'과 '코멘트' 간에 불일치하는 평가 범주 1개를 찾아야 함. 불일치란: 점수는 높은데 코멘트가 실제 오류/문제를 언급하거나, 점수는 낮은데 코멘트가 잘됐다고 하는 경우. 세 범주(Content, Organization, Grammar)를 각각 점수 ↔ 코멘트로 대조한다.

steps.label: Student 1 Organization 검토 → 불일치 확인
steps.explanation: Organization: Good to Fair. 코멘트: 'ideas were smoothly connected using appropriate linking words'. 실제 글 확인: 'It was spicy. It was very delicious. It took some time to order.' → 모두 단순 나열 문장, linking words 전혀 없음. 코멘트는 linking words를 칭찬하지만 실제 글에는 연결어가 없음 → Organization 코멘트와 실제 글 불일치 ✗

steps.label: Student 1 Grammar 검토 → 일치 확인
steps.explanation: Grammar: Excellent. 코멘트: 'There were no major grammatical errors that obscured the meaning.' 실제 글 문법 확인: 문장들이 단순하지만 문법적 오류 없음. 'no major grammatical errors' = Excellent 등급과 일치 ✓ → Grammar는 불일치 아님.

steps.label: Student 2 Content/Organization 검토 → 일치 확인
steps.explanation: Content: Excellent + 'message was very clear, good details' → 일치 ✓. Organization: Excellent + 'good connections, easy to follow' + concluding sentence 있음('it was worth visiting') → 일치 ✓. Content·Organization 코멘트와 점수 모두 일치.

steps.label: Student 2 Grammar 검토 → 불일치 확인
steps.explanation: Grammar 점수: Good to Fair. 코멘트: 'errors in the use of articles'만 언급. 실제 글의 오류: 'I have to wait long' (→ had to wait; 시제 오류), 'I eat the pizza quickly' (→ ate; 시제 오류), 'Restaurant was very crowded' (→ The restaurant; 관사 오류). 코멘트는 article 오류만 언급하지만 실제로는 시제 오류 등 다수. 코멘트가 실제 오류 범위를 충분히 반영하지 못함 → Grammar 코멘트 불일치 ✗

key_terms.definition_en: A writing assessment method that evaluates multiple distinct categories (e.g., content, organization, grammar) separately, each with its own score. Allows detailed diagnostic feedback but requires that scores and written comments be mutually consistent within each category.
key_terms.definition_ko: 분석적 채점 — 내용, 구성, 문법 등 여러 범주를 별도 점수로 평가하는 쓰기 평가 방식. 세부 진단 피드백이 가능하나 각 범주 내 점수와 코멘트 간 일관성이 요구됨.
key_terms.exam_usage: 점수 범주(Content/Organization/Grammar)와 코멘트 간 불일치 판단의 근거

key_terms.definition_en: A quality of assessment in which written comments accurately reflect the scores assigned, allowing learners to understand what a grade means and act on it. When a score and its accompanying comment are contradictory — e.g., an 'Excellent' grade paired with comments implying errors exist — feedback loses its diagnostic value.
key_terms.definition_ko: 피드백 일관성 — 서면 코멘트가 부여된 점수와 정확히 대응되는 평가의 질적 속성. 점수와 코멘트가 모순될 경우(예: Excellent 점수에 오류를 암시하는 코멘트) 피드백의 진단적 가치를 잃음.
key_terms.exam_usage: 문제 핵심: Mr. Kim의 코멘트가 점수와 불일치하는 범주를 찾는 것

key_terms.definition_en: A scoring category measuring the correctness of grammatical forms including verb tense, agreement, article use, and sentence structure. An 'Excellent' grade implies near-absence of errors; 'Good to Fair' indicates noticeable but non-impeding errors.
key_terms.definition_ko: 쓰기에서의 문법 정확성 — 시제, 일치, 관사 사용, 문장 구조 등 문법 형식의 정확도를 측정하는 채점 범주. Excellent는 오류가 거의 없어야 하며 Good to Fair는 의사소통을 방해하지 않는 수준의 오류를 허용함.
key_terms.exam_usage: Student 2(Good to Fair 점수 vs. article 오류만 언급한 코멘트 — 시제 오류 누락)의 불일치 근거; Student 1은 Grammar 일치(Excellent + 오류 없음), Organization이 불일치

model_answer.essay_content: Student 1's Organization comment is inconsistent: Mr. Kim states that ideas were smoothly connected using appropriate linking words, but the actual review contains only simple, unconnected sentences with no linking words at all. Student 2's Grammar comment is inconsistent: Mr. Kim mentions only article errors, but the text also contains tense errors such as "I eat" and "I have to wait," which are not addressed in the feedback.

scoring_criteria.criterion: Student 1 근거: 코멘트는 linking words 칭찬하지만 실제 글에 linking words 없음
Student 2 근거: article 외에도 시제 오류('I eat', 'I have to wait') 있는데 코멘트가 article만 언급

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Student 1's Organization comment is inconsistent: Mr. Kim praises the use of appropriate linking words, but the review consists only of short, simple sentences with no connectors at all. Student 2's Grammar comment is inconsistent: although Mr. Kim mentions only article errors, the text also includes tense errors such as 'I eat' and 'I have to wait,' which go unaddressed.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: For Student 1, Organization is misaligned — Mr. Kim's comment claims ideas were smoothly connected with linking words, but no linking words appear in the actual writing. For Student 2, Grammar is misaligned — the comment focuses only on article errors while tense errors like 'I eat the pizza quickly' and 'I have to wait' are overlooked.

references.author: Brown, H. D. & Abeywickrama, P.
references.title: Language Assessment: Principles and Classroom Practices (3rd ed.)
references.excerpt: In analytic scoring, each criterion must be evaluated independently and the accompanying written feedback should accurately reflect the score assigned in order to preserve validity and provide actionable guidance.

references.author: Weigle, S. C.
references.title: Assessing Writing
references.excerpt: Analytic scoring evaluates writing along multiple separate dimensions — such as content, organization, vocabulary, language use, and mechanics — assigning a score to each. Unlike holistic scoring, analytic scoring allows raters and students to identify specific strengths and weaknesses. Crucially, the written feedback comments should be consistent with the subscores assigned; inconsistency between scores and commentary undermines the diagnostic value of the assessment and reduces rater credibility.

source.concept_explanation:
Analytic writing assessment evaluates Content, Organization, and Grammar as separate dimensions, each with its own score and feedback. For assessment to be valid, scores and comments must be consistent within each category: an 'Excellent' grammar score should not be accompanied by comments implying errors exist, and a 'Good to Fair' comment should account for all observable error types in the student's text.
  (분석적 쓰기 평가는 내용·구성·문법을 별도 차원으로 평가하며 각각 점수와 피드백을 부여한다. 평가의 타당성을 위해 점수와 코멘트는 범주 내에서 일관되어야 한다: Excellent 문법 점수는 오류 존재를 암시하는 코멘트와 함께 제시될 수 없으며, Good to Fair 코멘트는 학생 글에 존재하는 모든 오류 유형을 반영해야 한다.)

Feedback consistency: the extent to which written comments accurately reflect the scores assigned in each category. Inconsistency — an 'Excellent' grade with comments implying errors, or a 'Good to Fair' comment that omits visible errors — reduces the diagnostic and instructional value of assessment feedback (Brown & Abeywickrama, 2019).

Note: Washback (backwash) refers to the effect of testing on teaching and learning behavior — a separate concept from score-comment consistency.
